name: refugee_credential_verification_blockchain_delay_jobs
premise: Workforce board chair Casey Irving must decide whether to require blockchain-verified credentials for refugee skilled-trades placements starting Monday, a rule that reduces credential fraud by twenty-two percent in pilots but delays placements an average of eleven days. Advocate Kato Vance shows delays push families into illegal cash labor. Keeping the rule protects union members from undercutting by fraudulent certs. Employers threaten to hire off-platform if the board waives verification. The NGO partner warns that each extra week of verification pushes families below the threshold for subsidized housing placement in the host city. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Labor Policy.

agents.name: Casey Irving
agents.identity: I am the workforce board chair balancing employer trust, union apprenticeship standards, and refugee economic self-sufficiency metrics. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want fraud resistance without trapping refugees in limbo while rent comes due. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

agents.name: Kato Vance
agents.identity: I am a refugee advocate who tracks placement delays correlated with verification stacks and predatory off-platform hiring. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want a ninety-day waiver with manual sworn affidavits for refugees already vetted by federal resettlement partners. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

choices: I will enforce blockchain verification without waiver and fund emergency rent vouchers for delayed families, accepting voucher budget caps if demand spikes.
I will waive blockchain for ninety days for resettlement-partner attestations with random post-hoc audits, accepting elevated fraud risk and union criticism.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Employers and advocates are seated in the same hearing room awaiting Casey Irving's ruling. Casey Irving must choose between strict blockchain verification with rent vouchers or a ninety-day waiver with audits. What does Casey Irving do?
